title: Apify Review 2026: Pricing, Pros, Cons and Is It Worth It?
desc: Honest Apify review for 2026: real pricing from free to $999, how compute units and pay-per-result Actors work, pros, cons, alternatives and who should buy it.
keywords: apify review, apify review 2026, apify pricing, is apify worth it, apify alternatives, web scraping tools, apify actors, apify for lead generation
cta: Want scraped leads without learning the tooling?
body:
Quick verdict: Apify is the best all-round web scraping platform you can buy in 2026, because you almost never have to build anything. Its marketplace has more than 52,000 pre-built scrapers, called Actors, that pull data from Google Maps, LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok, Amazon and thousands of other sites within minutes of signing up. The free plan includes $5 of usage every month with no credit card, and the $29 Starter plan covers most freelancers and small lead-gen teams. The honest catch: costs are usage-based and hard to predict until you run a test, unused credits expire every month, and the jump from the $199 Scale plan to the $999 Business plan is steep. If you want scraped data without maintaining scrapers, Apify is the safest pick. If you only need clean markdown for an AI app, or you refuse to touch anything technical, a niche tool may fit better. Here is the full breakdown.

What is Apify?

Apify is a cloud platform for web scraping and automation. Instead of writing and hosting your own crawler, you run an Actor, a small serverless program that lives on Apify's infrastructure, does one job (scrape Google Maps results, extract Instagram profiles, monitor prices on Amazon) and saves the results to a dataset you can export as JSON, CSV or Excel, or push straight into Google Sheets, Make, Zapier or your CRM.

Three things make the platform stand out in 2026:

  • Apify Store: 52,000+ ready-made Actors built by Apify and a large developer community. For almost any site you want data from, someone has already built and maintains the scraper.
  • AI-native tooling: Apify runs an MCP server, so AI agents and assistants (Claude, custom GPT-style agents, internal copilots) can call Actors directly. If you are building agents that need live web data, this is one of the cleanest integrations available.
  • Serious infrastructure: scheduling, retries, rotating proxies, storage and an API on SOC 2 audited, GDPR compliant infrastructure. The European Commission uses it to monitor 800+ retailers for compliance.

Apify pricing in 2026: what each plan really includes

Apify uses a prepaid-credits model: every plan includes a monthly usage allowance you spend on Actor runs, proxies and storage, and you pay as you go beyond it. Current pricing, verified on Apify's own site in June 2026:

  • Free, $0: $5 of platform usage per month, $0.20 per compute unit, 16 GB max Actor RAM, 25 concurrent runs, 5 datacenter proxy IPs, community support. No credit card required.
  • Starter, $29 per month: $29 of usage included, $0.20 per compute unit, 64 GB RAM, 32 concurrent runs, 30 datacenter IPs, chat support and a Bronze discount on paid Store Actors.
  • Scale, $199 per month: $199 of usage, cheaper compute at $0.16 per unit, 256 GB RAM, 128 concurrent runs, 200 datacenter IPs, priority chat support, Silver Store discount and 1 hour of personal training per quarter.
  • Business, $999 per month: $999 of usage, $0.13 per compute unit, 512 GB RAM, 256 concurrent runs, 500 datacenter IPs, a dedicated account manager and the Gold Store discount.

Annual billing takes 10 percent off. Students get 30 percent off Starter and Scale, and eligible startups get 30 percent off Scale. There is also a Creator plan that gives developers $500 of free usage for 6 months to build and publish their own Actors, and Actor developers as a group earned over $1.4 million in payouts in a single recent month, which tells you the marketplace is alive and maintained.

The plan-picking rule is simple: start free, test the exact Actors you need, then buy the smallest plan whose credits cover your real usage. Create a free Apify account and run your first Actor in about 10 minutes, no credit card needed.

What changed since our June 2026 review

We first published this review in June 2026 and re-verified every price and limit against Apify's own pricing page on July 22, 2026. Two things moved, and we have updated the numbers above to match:

  • The Store grew from about 36,000 Actors to more than 52,000. That is even more ready-made scrapers to reach for before you ever write a line of code.
  • Apify doubled the included Actor RAM on every plan. Free now gives 16 GB (was 8 GB), Starter 64 GB (was 32 GB), Scale 256 GB (was 128 GB) and Business 512 GB (was 256 GB). More memory means heavier browser-based crawls fit inside your plan before you pay for the add-on RAM at $1 per GB.

Everything else held steady: the four plan prices, the $0.20, $0.16 and $0.13 compute-unit rates, the prepaid-credit model and the monthly credit expiry are all unchanged from June. Our verdict and rankings below are unchanged too, because none of the facts that drove them changed.

The real cost: compute units, Actor fees and proxies

This is the part most reviews skip, and it is where budgets go wrong. Your monthly bill is made of up to three layers:

  • Compute units (CU): 1 CU equals 1 GB of RAM used for 1 hour. A typical lightweight scrape burns a fraction of a CU; a heavy browser-based crawl can burn many. At $0.20 per CU on Starter, $29 of credits buys 145 compute units, which is plenty for most lead-list and monitoring jobs.
  • Paid Store Actors: many community Actors charge their own fee on top of compute, either a monthly rental or pay-per-result (for example, a fixed price per 1,000 scraped items). The price is always shown on the Actor's page, and your plan's Bronze, Silver or Gold discount reduces it.
  • Proxies and storage: datacenter IPs are included in every plan, but heavily protected sites need residential proxies at $8 per GB on Starter ($7 to $7.50 on higher tiers), and SERP proxies cost $2.50 per 1,000 Google results. Storage adds cents, not dollars, for typical use.

Two gotchas to respect: unused prepaid credits expire at the end of each billing cycle, they never roll over, and overage is billed automatically to your next invoice once you pass your allowance (free users are simply paused until the next month). The fix is boring but effective: run a one-week test on the free plan, check the usage chart in your billing console, multiply by your real volume, and size your plan from data instead of guessing.

What Apify gets right

  • You skip the build entirely. For Google Maps, LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok, Twitter/X, Amazon and thousands of other targets, a maintained Actor already exists. When sites change their layout, the Actor developer fixes it, not you.
  • Genuinely free entry. $5 of monthly credits with full Store access is enough to test real jobs before spending anything, and no competitor at this scale matches that.
  • It grows with you. The same platform serves a freelancer scraping 500 leads a month and an enterprise crawling millions of pages. Groupon doubled lead volume on it, and Intercom's Fin has resolved 28 million+ AI chats using Apify-sourced data.
  • Best-in-class integrations. Native connectors for Google Sheets, Make, Zapier, n8n and webhooks, a clean REST API, the open-source Crawlee library if you want code-level control, and the MCP server for AI agents.
  • Anti-blocking that works. Built-in proxy rotation, browser fingerprinting and retries mean far fewer dead runs than DIY scripts or thin API wrappers.

If that solves a problem you have this week, start with the free plan and upgrade to Starter only when your usage proves it.

The honest downsides

  • Costs are unpredictable until you test. Usage-based pricing means two similar-looking Actors can cost very different amounts per 1,000 results. Always test-run before committing to volume.
  • Credits expire monthly. Pay for $199 of usage, consume $90, and the remaining $109 vanishes at cycle end. Size your plan down, not up.
  • The Scale to Business gap is brutal. $199 jumps straight to $999 with nothing in between except add-ons (extra RAM at $1 per GB, extra concurrency at $5 per run), which only partly bridge it.
  • Community Actor quality varies. Top Actors are excellent and actively maintained; the long tail is hit or miss. Check the Actor's rating, user count and last-updated date before relying on one.
  • Custom work needs a developer. If no Actor covers your target site, you are writing JavaScript or Python (or hiring someone who does). Point-and-click tools are easier for true no-code users.
  • Compliance is on you. Apify gives you the tools, but scraping personal data still has GDPR and terms-of-service implications. Scrape responsibly, especially for cold outreach.

Apify vs the alternatives in 2026

No single scraping tool wins everywhere. Here is the honest map:

  • Apify vs Octoparse: Octoparse is a visual, no-code desktop scraper that is easier for non-technical users on arbitrary sites, but it is weaker at scale and its officially supported templates cover far fewer sites than Apify's 52,000+ Actors.
  • Apify vs Browse AI: Browse AI's point-and-click robots are great under roughly 10,000 rows a month and very beginner friendly. Past that volume, or for complex multi-step jobs, Apify is cheaper and more robust.
  • Apify vs Firecrawl: Firecrawl does one thing brilliantly, turning pages into LLM-ready markdown via API. But independent benchmarks show it struggling on heavily protected sites, where Apify's proxy stack and specialized Actors hold up much better. Many AI teams use both.
  • Apify vs Bright Data: Bright Data wins for enterprise proxy infrastructure and compliance-heavy datasets, at enterprise prices and complexity. For everyone below that tier, Apify is simpler and cheaper.

The pattern: specialists beat Apify in one dimension each, but as the single platform that covers pre-built scrapers, custom code, scheduling, proxies and AI-agent access at once, Apify has no real equal right now.

Using Apify for lead generation (how we actually use it)

Most of our readers care about one use case: filling the pipeline. Apify is the engine behind a lot of modern B2B prospecting, and the workflow looks like this:

  • Scrape a niche source with an Actor: Google Maps for local businesses, LinkedIn for roles and companies, directories and review sites for verticals.
  • Enrich and verify the list with your data tools, then dedupe against your CRM.
  • Feed the verified list into your cold email system with personalization pulled from the scraped fields.

We run exactly this stack for clients at our lead generation service, and pair it with managed sending through our cold email marketing service when clients want the whole pipeline handled. If you would rather buy the outcome than learn the tooling, that is the shortcut. If you want the full tool landscape first, see our guide to the best B2B lead scraping tools in 2026.

Final verdict: who should (and should not) buy Apify

Buy Apify if you need data from well-known platforms without building scrapers, you run lead generation or market research at any real volume, you are technical enough to read a usage dashboard, or you are building AI agents that need live web data through MCP.

Skip it if you only ever need a few hundred rows from one simple site (Browse AI or a free tool covers that), you want pure no-code on obscure sites (Octoparse), or your only job is feeding markdown to an LLM at small scale (Firecrawl's free tier).

Rating: 4.5 out of 5. The marketplace model, the genuinely useful free plan and the AI-agent integrations outweigh the pricing opacity and the Scale-to-Business cliff. Try Apify free, no credit card required, test your exact use case against the $5 monthly credit, and let the usage data tell you which plan, if any, you need.
faqs: Apify is a cloud web scraping and automation platform. People use it to extract data from websites like Google Maps, LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok and Amazon using 52,000+ pre-built scrapers called Actors, then export the results to JSON, CSV, Excel, Google Sheets or their CRM via API and integrations. It is widely used for lead generation, market research, price monitoring and feeding live web data to AI agents.
How much does Apify cost in 2026?

Apify has four plans in 2026: Free ($0 with $5 of monthly usage credits), Starter ($29 per month), Scale ($199 per month) and Business ($999 per month). Each plan includes that amount as prepaid platform usage, with pay-as-you-go billing beyond it. Annual billing is 10 percent cheaper, students get 30 percent off Starter and Scale, and some paid Store Actors charge their own per-result or rental fees on top.

Yes. The free plan includes $5 of platform usage every month, full access to the Apify Store, 16 GB of Actor RAM and 5 datacenter proxy IPs, with no credit card required. That is enough to run real test scrapes. When the credit runs out, free accounts pause until the next month, so you can never be surprise-billed on the free tier.
What is an Apify compute unit?

A compute unit (CU) is Apify's billing unit for processing power: 1 CU equals 1 GB of memory used for 1 hour. Compute units cost $0.20 on the Free and Starter plans, $0.16 on Scale and $0.13 on Business. A lightweight API-based scrape uses a small fraction of a CU, while heavy browser-based crawls with large memory allocations consume more, which is why testing your exact Actor first is the only reliable way to predict costs.
Is Apify good for lead generation?

Yes, it is one of the strongest tools for B2B lead generation. Actors can scrape Google Maps listings, LinkedIn data, business directories and niche review sites, and the results flow into enrichment and cold email tools via native integrations. The typical stack is: scrape with Apify, verify and enrich the list, then send through a cold email platform. Agencies, including ours, run exactly this pipeline for clients.
What are the best Apify alternatives?

The main alternatives in 2026 are Octoparse (visual no-code desktop scraping), Browse AI (point-and-click robots, best under about 10,000 rows a month), Firecrawl (API that turns pages into LLM-ready markdown for AI apps) and Bright Data (enterprise proxy and dataset infrastructure). Each beats Apify in one niche, but none matches its combination of 52,000+ pre-built scrapers, custom code support, scheduling, proxies and AI-agent integrations on one platform.
